description: Figure 11 presents a model prediction validation chart comparing actual yield strength (YS) values against machine learning-predicted counterparts for medium-manganese low-density steels. The line graph displays two distinct curves: a gray line representing experimentally measured Actual YS and a red line representing GA-Extra Trees Predicted YS across approximately 120 sample indices. The vertical axis spans 0–3000 MPa, encompassing yield strength values from diverse alloy compositions including Fe-0.25C-3Mn-2Al-0.4Si and literature datasets. The predicted curve closely follows the actual data trend with minimal deviation, visually confirming the model's high accuracy reported in the statistical validation (R²=0.997, RMSE=31.60 MPa). This comparison demonstrates that the Genetic Algorithm-optimized Extremely Randomized Trees framework successfully integrates contributions from solid solution, grain refinement, dislocation, and Orowan strengthening mechanisms to predict mechanical properties of steels subjected to varying annealing temperatures (750–850°C) and thermomechanical processing histories.
